name: Gale Wade
ai_summary: 20-year-old with three years of childcare experience across daycares and nanny agencies, currently studying nursing to become a pediatric nurse. Located in Lawrenceville, GA with a 30-mile drive, seeking full-time work; highlights patience, calmness under pressure, and relationship-building as classroom strengths.

transcripts.q3:
Hi, my name is Gil Wade. I am 20 years old. I have about three years of childcare. Experience working in setting such as daycares I worked for a nanny agency. And I've also worked with like one-on-one with families that have experience with kids ages 0 to 13 years old. And the reason I enjoy working with children is because I like to be a part of their learning and their growth development and I find it very rewarding.
 To help them understand new things, build their confidence and see their progress over time and children are just such a bundle of joy. It is just so exciting to be around them. They truly do make life just that much better. And I just I really enjoy it. And I'm also currently right now in school for nursing to be a pediatric nurse. I really truly feel like working with them is a part of my passion and I want to do it.
 For the rest of my life. Thank you.
transcripts.q4: So I am in Lawrenceville Georgia, I would be willing to travel up to 30 minutes for a childcare, a job.
transcripts.q5: I would definitely say that I'm looking for 40 hours a week, so definitely full time.
transcripts.q6:
If there was to be a conflict between two children in my classroom, I would first make sure everyone is safe and that nobody is harming the other and separate them. If needed. If a child would hit another child, I would calmly, but very firmly, stop the behavior, right away, using very clear language. I cannot let you hit, do not hit, we do not head, then I would check on the child who was hurt and make sure they're okay, obviously after
 Sure that I would hope both children calm down and talk through what happened in a simple very age-appropriate way so that they can also understand me and why whatever is happening is wrong and that we can both work through this conflict. I would guide them towards better ways to express their feelings like using words or asking me for help rather than taking it into their own hands and hitting whatever it might be. I would also reinforce expectations and follow the classrooms Behavior policy. So the situation is handled
 Old correctly and consistently.
transcripts.q7:
I think I would make a great up to beat up kid teacher, because I'm a patient person. I'm very calm Under Pressure. I'm good. Building positive relationships with the children that I am around or responsible for I enjoy helping students understand things in a way that makes sense to them. And I'm able to adapt my Approach depending on each child's needs. I also care about creating a safe and supportive classroom, where students feel encouraged to learn and they want to learn, and they're having fun with learning.
 And like I said before, I truly just enjoy being around kids, I truly enjoy working with them. I truly enjoyed seeing their growth and their development and it is a passion of mine and that I've based my entire career around
